memoryArtifacts.headline: The Garretts -- Before America
memoryArtifacts.description:
The Garretts — Before America
by John E. Garrett
Last year, I published my work entitled, "1000 Years of Garrett Family History". The emphasis in that effort
was the genealogy of the Garretts since their immigration to America in the mid-seventeenth century. This
work examines how the Garretts evolved during the generations that they spent in England, Wales and
Ireland from the time the first ancestor landed in England until the first Garrett came to America.
My research involved the use of:
 A commercial genealogy website named Ancestry.com.
 Extensive use of the Google search engine on the Internet
The first 100 years, starting in the eleventh century, was the most interesting. That was where I found a
remarkable relationship between my ancestors and King Edward the Confessor, King William the Conqueror
and King Henry I. My first ancestor to land in England, Otho Geraldini, grew-up with Edward before he
became King; his son Walter FitzOtho was in William, the Bastard's army; and his son Gerald grew-up with
Prince Henry (King William"s son) who became King Henry I. Their close relationship is demonstrated by the
vast amount of land, castles and titles bestowed upon them.
In retrospect, I am impressed at how important the genes of the spouses are to the family's genealogy. The
best example of this is when my ancestor, Gerald of Windsor married Nesta Rhys, a Welsh Princess. There
offsprings were all outstanding. The next "high" came when the Garrett's became merchants in the 16th
century, opened trade with Russia, became Lord Mayor of London and honorary knighthood.
To make this work more readable, I have placed several interesting "asides" in the Appendix. For example,
the de Clares, of Richard "Strongbow" de Clare fame married into the Garretts. I found extensive genealogy
on the ancestry of Richard "Strongbow" de Clare going back before 100 AD. So, I put his data into two
appendices. One for the French ancestors and another for the Viking ancestors.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William FitzMaurice Baron of Naas於大約1150年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達納阿斯時，他的父親Lord of Lanstephan Maurice FitzGerald De Whyte XVIIII46歲，母親Alice Montgomery49歲。他於1174年與Aline de Clare結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭爾被列為justice。他去世於1199年9月之前在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: FitzMaurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When William FitzMaurice Baron of Naas was born about 1150, in Naas, County Kildare, Ireland, his father, Lord of Lanstephan Maurice FitzGerald De Whyte XVIIII, was 46 and his mother, Alice Montgomery, was 49. He married Aline de Clare in 1174. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as justice in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. He died before September 1199, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William FitzMaurice Baron of Naas est né environ 1150, à Naas, County Kildare, Irlande, son père, Lord of Lanstephan Maurice FitzGerald De Whyte XVIIII, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Alice Montgomery, avait 49 ans. Il a épousé Aline de Clare en 1174. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est justice à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé avant septembre 1199, dans sa ville natale.

Er hat 1174 Aline de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als justice in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb vor September 1199 in seinem Geburtsort.

ko.lifeSummary: William FitzMaurice Baron of Naas(이)가 대략 1150년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 나아스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord of Lanstephan Maurice FitzGerald De Whyte XVIIII(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Montgomery(은)는 49세였다. 그는 1174년 Aline de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 justice이었다. 그는 1199년 9월 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William FitzMaurice Baron of Naas родился около 1150 г. в местности наас, коунти каилдар, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Lord of Lanstephan Maurice FitzGerald De Whyte XVIIII было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Montgomery, 49. Он женился на женщине по имени Aline de Clare в 1174 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии justice в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер перед сентябрь 1199 г. в его родном городе.
